title: Process for producing fuel and/or chemicals from renewable sources
abstract: A process for hydroprocessing a renewable feedstock includes passing the renewable feedstock to a first catalyst zone comprising a monometallic Group VIB catalyst, under hydrodeoxygenation conditions, to produce a reaction mixture comprising a hydrotreated oil having an olefin content exceeding a thermodynamic equilibrium olefin content by a factor of at least 250 times. The reaction mixture is then passed to a second catalyst zone comprising a bimetallic catalyst having a Group VIII metal and a Group VIB metal, under hydrogenation conditions, to produce an effluent comprising a hydroprocessed oil having an olefin content proximate a thermodynamic equilibrium olefin content.
claims: Claims 1. A process for hydroprocessing a renewable feedstock, the process comprising the steps of: providing a renewable feedstock; passing the renewable feedstock to a first catalyst zone comprising a monometallic Group VIB catalyst, under hydrodeoxygenation conditions, to produce a first reaction mixture comprising a hydrotreated oil having an olefin content exceeding a thermodynamic equilibrium olefin content by a factor of at least 250 times; and passing the first reaction mixture to a second catalyst zone comprising a bimetallic catalyst having a Group VIII metal and a Group VIB metal, under hydrogenation conditions, to produce an effluent comprising a hydroprocessed oil having an olefin content proximate a thermodynamic equilibrium olefin content. 2. The process of claim 1, wherein the hydrotreated oil has an olefin content exceeding a thermodynamic equilibrium olefin content by a factor of at least 500 times. 3. The process of claim 1, wherein the hydrotreated oil has a reference olefin content of at least 125 ppm-w. 4. The process of claim 1, wherein the hydroprocessed oil has a reference olefin content in a range of from is 0.5 ppm-w to 5 ppm-w. 5. The process of claim 1, wherein the volumetric amount of monometallic catalyst is greater that the volumetric amount of bimetallic catalyst. 6. The process of claim 1, wherein the volumetric ratio of monometallic:bimetallic catalysts is in a range of from 60:40 to 95:5, preferably in a range of from 70:30 to 90: 10. 7. The process of claim 1, further comprising the step of separating the hydroprocessed oil from vapour phase present in the effluent from the second catalyst zone. 8. The process of claim 7, wherein the separating step comprises a high-pressure separator. 9. The process of claim 7, wherein the mercaptan content of the separated oil has a reference olefin content of at most 40 ppm-w, preferably at most 15 ppm-w. 10. The process of claim 1, wherein the renewable feedstock is selected from the group consisting of one or more bio-renewable fats and oils, liquid derived from a biomass liquefaction process, liquid derived from a waste liquefaction process, and combinations thereof. 11. The process of claim 1, further comprising adding a petroleum-derived feedstock for co-processing with the renewable feedstock, preferably in an amount to produce a feed stream comprising from 5 to 30 wt.% renewable feedstock. 12. The process of claim 7, further comprising the step of passing the hydroprocessed oil to a hydroisomerization step. 13. The process of claim 7, further comprising the step of passing the hydroprocessed oil to a steam cracking step.
